clean_text:
CAUVERY NEERAVARI NIGAM LIMITED
(A Government of Karnataka Undertaking)
Registered Office: Cauvery Bhavan, Kempe Gowda Road, Bengaluru   560 009
Divisional Office: Mysuru Irrigation Division, No. 14, KRS Road, Mysuru   570 010
NOTICE INVITING TENDER (e-NIT)
NIT No.: CNNL/MYS-DIV/DS/2026-27/007
Date: 10 June 2026
TENDER FOR DESILTING OF VISVESVARAYA CANAL   MYSURU REACH (PACKAGE DS-07)
Chainage: Km 96.400 to Km 110.700 (14.3 km)
1. WORK DETAILS
Cauvery Neeravari Nigam Limited (CNNL), Mysuru Irrigation Division, invites
online tenders from eligible contractors for the following work:
Work Name    : Desilting of Visvesvaraya Canal (Mysuru Reach), Package DS-07
Location     : Chainage Km 96.400 to Km 110.700, Mysuru District, Karnataka
Scope        : Mechanical and manual desilting of the main canal bed and banks,
               disposal of silt, restoration of embankment slopes (1:1.5),
               repair and re-plastering of CC lining (grade M15) at 23 locations,
               reconstruction of 4 cross-drainage structures (sluice type),
               vegetation clearance on canal banks (both sides, 14.3 km),
               and desilting of 6 field channels (each 0.8 1.4 km).
Estimated Cost   : Rs. 4,10,00,000/- (Rs. Four Crore Ten Lakh)
EMD              : Rs. 82,000/- (Rs. Eighty-Two Thousand)   2% of estimated cost
Tender Fee       : Rs. 2,000/- (non-refundable, paid online)
Performance Security : 5% of accepted contract value
Defect Liability Period : 12 months from date of substantial completion
2. IMPORTANT DATES
Publication of e-NIT          : 10 June 2026
Last date for document download : 05 July 2026, 17:30 hrs
Last date for clarifications  : 08 July 2026
Pre-Bid Meeting               : 10 July 2026, 11:00 hrs at CNNL Mysuru Divisional Office
Last date for bid submission  : 22 July 2026, 17:00 hrs
Opening of Technical Bids     : 24 July 2026, 11:00 hrs
Bid validity                  : 120 days from date of submission
Bids must be submitted online on the Karnataka e-Procurement portal
(https://eproc.karnataka.gov.in). Physical submission not accepted.
3. SCOPE OF WORK   DETAIL

3.1 Canal Desilting
  The accumulated silt, organic debris, and weed growth in the canal bed and
  banks shall be removed using the following methods:
  (a) Mechanical desilting using excavator + tipper (for main canal bed)
  (b) Manual desilting and basket removal (for reaches inaccessible to machinery)
  Excavated silt to be disposed at designated spoil sites approved by CNNL.
3.2 Embankment Restoration
  Embankment cross-sections shall be restored to design dimensions (FSL, bank
  width 2.4 m, side slope 1:1.5). Settlement or erosion pockets to be made
  good with compacted earth (proctor density   95%).
3.3 Concrete Lining Repair
  Existing M15 CC lining to be repaired at 23 damaged locations (total area
  approx. 1,850 sqm). Repair shall include:
  (a) Removing loose/cracked panels
  (b) Sub-grade compaction
  (c) Providing 75 mm M15 CC lining with 3 mm plaster finish
  (d) Expansion joints with bitumen felt at 6 m centres
3.4 Cross-Drainage Structures
  Reconstruction of 4 cross-drainage sluices (rectangular box type, 0.6 m
  opening, head wall and tail wall in M20 CC, wooden shutters). Co-ordination
  with CNNL's hydraulic engineer for gate setting.
3.5 Field Channel Desilting
  Manual desilting of 6 field channels (total 6.2 km). Silt disposal at
  farmer's field or spoil site as directed by field engineer.
4. ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA
IMPORTANT: Bids from contractors not meeting ALL the eligibility criteria listed
below will be summarily rejected at the technical evaluation stage. Waivers
will not be granted.
4.1 Annual Turnover
  The bidder shall have an Average Annual Turnover not less than Rs. 2.00 Crore
  from civil or irrigation works during the last 3 (three) financial years
  (2023-24, 2024-25, 2025-26).
  Supporting documents: Audited Profit & Loss Statement and Balance Sheet for
  last 3 financial years, certified by a Chartered Accountant.
4.2 Technical Experience   Similar Canal / Irrigation Works

  The bidder shall have successfully completed at least 1 (one) similar canal
  work or irrigation scheme work during the last 5 (five) years (on or after
  01 April 2021), of value not less than Rs. 1.50 Crore.
  "Similar work" means: Desilting, embankment repair, or canal lining work on
  an irrigation canal, distributary, or field channel maintained by an
  Irrigation Department, CNNL, KNNL, CADA, or equivalent organisation.
  Works involving road embankments, building construction, or drainage of
  non-irrigation structures shall NOT qualify as similar work for this purpose.
  Supporting documents: Work order + Performance / Completion certificate from
  the concerned Irrigation Department or parastatal, specifying the nature,
  value, and date of completion of the work.
4.3 Contractor Registration
  The bidder shall hold a valid registration as Class II Contractor or above
  (Class I, Special Class) with:
  (a) Karnataka Public Works Department (KPWD), OR
  (b) Karnataka Rural Infrastructure Development Limited (KRIDL), OR
  (c) CNNL / KNNL (irrigation department registration), OR
  (d) Central Water Commission (CWC) / CPWD equivalent category
  A road-only PWD registration without an irrigation/hydraulic works endorsement
  does not satisfy this condition.
4.4 Other Documents Required
  (a) Valid GST Registration (GSTIN)
  (b) PAN
  (c) EPF / ESIC Registration
  (d) Undertaking that no litigation / arbitration is pending with any
      Irrigation Department in Karnataka
5. EVALUATION CRITERIA
5.1 Technical bids will be evaluated on pass/fail basis against Section 4.
    Only technically qualified bidders will proceed to financial bid opening.
5.2 Financial bids will be evaluated on L1 (lowest valid bid) basis.
    Bids above 10% of estimated cost shall not be considered for award.
5.3 CNNL reserves the right to award work to L2 if L1 fails to execute
    the agreement within 7 working days of letter of award.
6. SITE CONDITIONS
6.1 Canal water shall be shut down for the work season (1 October to 31 March).

    All desilting work must be completed within this window.
6.2 Contractors must arrange own water supply for curing of concrete works.
    No potable water supply from CNNL will be provided.
6.3 Machinery mobilisation: access roads to the canal bank are kachcha
    (unpaved) and may require minor repair for heavy vehicles. Contractor
    to include mobilisation cost in their rates.
6.4 The canal traverses agricultural land. Contractors must coordinate
    with CNNL Field Engineer to minimise crop damage. Any crop damage
    compensation is the contractor's liability.
7. GENERAL TERMS
7.1 All works to be executed as per KPWD Specifications for Irrigation Works
    (2018 edition) and IS codes as applicable.
7.2 CNNL's Engineer-in-Charge's decision on quality and measurement is final.
7.3 Subletting of work (in full or in part) is not permitted.
7.4 The successful bidder must deploy a resident site engineer (Diploma in
    Civil Engineering, minimum 3 years irrigation works experience).
7.5 CNNL reserves the right to cancel or postpone the tender at any stage
    without assigning any reason.
7.6 Disputes are subject to jurisdiction of courts in Mysuru District.
8. CONTACT
Divisional Engineer (Canals)
CNNL Mysuru Irrigation Division
No. 14, KRS Road, Mysuru   570 010
Email: de-canals.mys@cnnl.karnataka.gov.in
Phone: 0821-2440-122
END OF NOTICE INVITING TENDER
NIT No.: CNNL/MYS-DIV/DS/2026-27/007
